title: Autodesk, Inc. (ADSK) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Autodesk, Inc. (ADSK) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1. Economic Prospect Score: 77/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 12.0% growth rate is supported by its highly recurring SaaS revenue model, consistent pricing power, and expanding penetration of cloud services in the construction sector.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.8% discount rate balances the high quality, recurring nature of its software revenue against the inherent cyclicality of its end markets (construction and manufacturing).

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.5% terminal rate is justified by Autodesk's entrenched position as an industry standard and the ongoing, long-term secular trend of digitizing global infrastructure.

thesisTitle: The Toll Bridge for Global Infrastructure
thesisParagraphs: Autodesk's software is the foundational layer for modern architecture and engineering. You cannot build a skyscraper or design a complex machine without it. This provides the company with near-absolute switching costs and extraordinary pricing power.

The ongoing shift toward cloud-based collaboration (Autodesk Construction Cloud) and the integration of AI will only deepen its entrenchment. Despite near-term macroeconomic sensitivities, the long-term cash generation profile makes the current valuation an attractive entry point.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via Gemini Analysis (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Is 12% growth sustainable?
faq.answer: Yes. Autodesk's growth is driven by a combination of new user adoption (especially in emerging markets), migrating legacy users to more comprehensive cloud platforms, and regular pricing increases.

faq.question: How vulnerable is Autodesk to a recession?
faq.answer: While new license sales may slow during a recession, their subscription model ensures that existing revenue is highly insulated. Most firms will cut headcount before they cancel their Autodesk licenses.

faq.question: Why is it undervalued?
faq.answer: The market occasionally penalizes Autodesk for short-term cyclical fears in the construction sector, underestimating the sheer durability and compounding nature of its SaaS free cash flows.

prospectVerdictDetail: Autodesk commands a massive economic moat as the industry standard for architecture, engineering, and construction (AEC) software. Its successful transition to a SaaS model ensures highly recurring, high-margin revenue. While macro headwinds in construction can cause near-term volatility, its indispensable software and increasing integration of AI tools position it for sustained long-term compounding.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Autodesk maintains strong momentum driven by its transition to cloud-based platforms and the ongoing digitization of the construction industry.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Autodesk delivers consistent double-digit top-line growth, outpacing many legacy software peers as it monetizes its user base more effectively through cloud services.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: It dominates the AEC design software market. Growth is primarily driven by upselling existing customers to comprehensive platform solutions like Autodesk Construction Cloud.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: The company possesses significant pricing power, evidenced by its ability to regularly implement price increases and migrate users to more expensive subscription tiers.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Autodesk continually refines its core offerings and is actively integrating generative AI capabilities, although the core functionality of tools like AutoCAD evolves deliberately.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Autodesk's moat is exceptionally wide. Its software is deeply embedded in the workflows of architects and engineers worldwide, creating immense switching costs.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Switching costs are absolute. Retraining entire firms on new software, migrating complex legacy files, and disrupting established collaborative workflows is often financially unviable.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: Strong network effects exist within the industry; standardizing on Autodesk software facilitates collaboration between different contractors, architects, and engineers on massive projects.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Autodesk holds significant IP in digital design and simulation. Its file formats (like .dwg) are de facto industry standards.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: As a pure software/SaaS company, it requires minimal physical capital expenditures, leading to massive free cash flow generation and high returns on invested capital.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Sentiment is generally bullish, recognizing its entrenched position, though occasionally tempered by cyclical concerns in global construction and manufacturing.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Estimates remain strong as the company continues to demonstrate operating leverage and margin expansion through its SaaS model.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative is shifting positively toward its AI initiatives, viewing Autodesk as a prime beneficiary of generative AI in streamlining design and engineering workflows.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management has successfully navigated the complex transition to a subscription model and uses its significant free cash flow for strategic acquisitions and share repurchases.

prospectKeyRisks: A severe, prolonged global recession could significantly curtail commercial construction and infrastructure spending, impacting new license growth.
Increasing competition from nimble, cloud-native startups focusing on specific niches within the AEC workflow.
Challenges in smoothly integrating numerous acquired technologies into a unified cloud platform.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Clean resolution of the accounting investigation and several quarters of credible financial reporting could lift the governance discount and restore the stock's premium SaaS multiple
Infrastructure spending from the US Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act and similar global programs creates a multi-year demand tailwind for civil engineering and infrastructure design software
AI-powered generative design in Autodesk tools could dramatically increase productivity for architects and engineers, driving higher willingness-to-pay and platform stickiness for premium tiers
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored ADSK at 84/100 and Opus at 72/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
